questions.question: What is React?
options: A JavaScript library for building user interfaces

questions.question: What is the smallest building block of a React application?
Which of the following is a syntax extension for JavaScript used in React?
How do you start a React project using Vite?
Which hook is used to manage state in a functional component?
How do you pass data from a parent component to a child component?
Which component in 'react-router-dom' is used to define a navigation link?
What is the Virtual DOM?
options: A direct copy of the real DOM
A lightweight representation of the real DOM in memory

questions.question: What does SPA stand for in web development?
How do you handle a click event in React?
In JSX, how many root elements can a component return?
'Props' is a shorthand representing which word?
Which JavaScript array method is commonly used to render lists in React?
What is typically the entry point file for a React application?
Which hook is used for performing side effects like data fetching?
How do you embed a JavaScript variable 'userName' inside a JSX tag?
What is the default port for a Vite-based development server?
How do you specify a CSS class in JSX?
What is a functional component?
options: A JavaScript function that returns JSX
A component with a class keyword

questions.question: Which operator is often used for simple conditional rendering in React?
What does the dependency array in 'useEffect' control?
options: The return value of the hook
When the effect function should be re-executed
The internal state of the component

questions.question: What happens when a component's state is updated?
options: The component and its children re-render

questions.question: Why is it important to use 'keys' when rendering lists of components?
options: To make the list look better
To help React identify which items have changed or been moved

questions.question: What is a 'controlled component' in React forms?
options: A component that cannot be edited
An input element whose value is controlled by React state

questions.question: Which hook from 'react-router-dom' allows you to access dynamic URL parameters?
What is a major difference between props and state?
options: Props are functional, state is structural
Props are read-only to the component, state is managed internally
State is passed down from parents, props are local

questions.question: When does 'useEffect(() => { ... }, [])' run?
options: Only once, after the initial render (on mount)

questions.question: Why can't you return multiple adjacent elements from a component without a wrapper?
options: JSX must be compiled into a single createElement call

questions.question: How do you prevent the default form submission behavior in React?
What is the 'onChange' attribute used for on an <input>?
options: To trigger a function when the input value changes

questions.question: How can you conditionally apply a CSS class based on a state 'isActive'?
Which component provides the context for navigation in React Router?
How should you update an object state variable 'user' with a new name?
What is 'component composition'?
options: Breaking a component into smaller pieces
The process of combining smaller components to build a larger UI

questions.question: What is a core 'Rule of Hooks'?
options: Call hooks only at the top level of React functions
Call hooks only in class components

questions.question: What is the purpose of a Fragment (<>...</>)?
options: To define a new component type
To group multiple elements without adding an extra node to the DOM

questions.question: Can you pass a function as a prop to a component?
options: No, props only support data types
Yes, just like any other value
Yes, but only in class components
Only if it is an arrow function

questions.question: What is one reason to favor functional components with hooks over class components?
options: Hooks allow better logic reuse and cleaner code
Functional components are only for simple UI

questions.question: What is a 'hook' in React?
options: A special function that lets functional components use state and lifecycle features

questions.question: How do you render a component <Alert /> only if 'hasError' is true?
How does the Virtual DOM optimize application performance?
options: By minimizing direct manipulation of the real DOM through a 'diffing' algorithm

questions.question: What is the purpose of the cleanup function returned by 'useEffect'?
options: To perform tasks like removing event listeners or canceling subscriptions before unmount

questions.question: What is 'lifting state up' in React?
options: Moving state to a lower-level component
Moving state to a common ancestor shared by components that need to share data

questions.question: How does React handle browser events to ensure cross-browser compatibility?
options: By wrapping native events into SyntheticEvents
By writing custom CSS for each browser

questions.question: What is 'Reconciliation' in React?
options: The process of merging two projects
The algorithm React uses to update the UI with new data in the Virtual DOM
The process of fixing merge conflicts

questions.question: Why is it discouraged to update state directly (e.g., this.state.val = 1)?
options: It does not trigger a re-render of the component
It is not valid JS syntax

questions.question: Which of these might cause a 'stale closure' issue in a 'useEffect' hook?
options: Referencing a state variable that isn't included in the dependency array

questions.question: How do you handle multiple input fields with one 'onChange' handler?
options: By using the [event.target.name] property to dynamically update the state key
By using different functions for each

questions.question: What is the primary function of <React.StrictMode>?
options: To make the code run faster
To help identify potential problems and double-render components in dev to detect side effects
To prevent users from using the console

questions.question: What is a main difference between 'useMemo' and 'useCallback'?
options: useMemo returns JSX, useCallback returns CSS
useMemo memoizes a value, useCallback memoizes a function instance
useMemo is for classes, useCallback for functions
They are identical in every way
